test_cases.description: Verify that the AuditEvent struct defines exactly 9 top-level fields with correct Go types and JSON struct tags.
preconditions: The `db` package at `internal/db` compiles successfully.
The `AuditEvent` struct is defined in `internal/db/events.go`.
test_cases.input: Inspect the AuditEvent struct definition via reflection or direct compilation check.
test_cases.expected: The struct has exactly 9 exported fields: ID (string, json:"id"), Timestamp (string, json:"timestamp"), RunID (string, json:"run_id"), EventType (string, json:"event_type"), NodeID (string, json:"node_id"), SessionID (string, json:"session_id"), Archetype (string, json:"archetype"), Severity (string, json:"severity"), Payload (json.RawMessage, json:"payload").
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: type_of_AuditEvent = reflect(db.AuditEvent); assert field_count(type_of_AuditEvent) == 9; assert field_type(type_of_AuditEvent, "ID") == string; assert json_tag(type_of_AuditEvent, "ID") == "id"; assert field_type(type_of_AuditEvent, "Payload") == json.RawMessage; assert json_tag(type_of_AuditEvent, "Payload") == "payload"

test_cases.description: Verify that the Payload field of AuditEvent is declared as json.RawMessage to preserve raw JSON bytes during unmarshalling.
preconditions: The `db` package at `internal/db` compiles successfully.
The `AuditEvent` struct is defined in `internal/db/events.go`.
test_cases.input: Unmarshal JSON `{"id":"abc","timestamp":"t","run_id":"r","event_type":"e","node_id":"","session_id":"","archetype":"","severity":"info","payload":{"k":"v"}}` into AuditEvent.
test_cases.expected: The Payload field contains the raw bytes `{"k":"v"}` without further parsing; the Go type of Payload is json.RawMessage ([]byte).
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: event = AuditEvent{}; json_unmarshal(body, &event); assert typeof(event.Payload) == json.RawMessage; assert string(event.Payload) == "{\"k\":\"v\"}" OR equivalent raw bytes

test_cases.description: Verify that the AuditEvent struct resides in the db package (internal/db) and its JSON tags allow json.Unmarshal to be used directly by both handler and db package.
preconditions: Source file `internal/db/events.go` exists and declares the AuditEvent struct.
Source file `internal/handler/events.go` imports `internal/db`.
test_cases.input: Compile the project; verify package declaration and import paths.
test_cases.expected: AuditEvent is declared in package `db` (internal/db/events.go); the handler imports and uses `db.AuditEvent` directly for json.Unmarshal; no separate DTO struct exists for audit events.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert package_of(db.AuditEvent) == "db"; assert handler_imports_contains("internal/db"); json_unmarshal(canonical_body, &db.AuditEvent{}) returns no error

test_cases.description: Verify that the POST /v1/events handler performs a single-pass json.Unmarshal of the body into AuditEvent, accepting a valid JSON object with all 9 fields.
preconditions: Service is running with a valid SQLite database initialised using the new 10-column events table schema.
Valid Bearer token is configured and used in the request.
Content-Type header is set to application/json.
test_cases.input: POST /v1/events with the canonical valid AuditEvent JSON body: {"id":"7c10ec9b-daaf-4146-8e40-6efc92e5db39","timestamp":"2026-06-18T11:26:26.527713+00:00","run_id":"20260618_112626_17dd3f","event_type":"run.start","node_id":"","session_id":"","archetype":"","severity":"info","payload":{"plan_hash":"abc123"}}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 Created with zero-byte body and no Content-Type header; one row inserted in the events table.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST("/v1/events", canonical_valid_body, auth_header); assert resp.status_code == 201; assert len(resp.body) == 0; assert resp.headers["Content-Type"] == ""; row = db_query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events WHERE id='7c10ec9b-daaf-4146-8e40-6efc92e5db39'"); assert row.count == 1

test_cases.description: Verify that the handler checks non-empty constraints on id, timestamp, run_id, event_type, and severity, while accepting empty strings for node_id, session_id, and archetype.
preconditions: Service is running with initialised events table.
Valid Bearer token and Content-Type: application/json are used.
test_cases.input: POST /v1/events with the canonical valid AuditEvent JSON where node_id, session_id, and archetype are all empty strings.
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 Created; one row stored with node_id='', session_id='', archetype='' in the database.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST("/v1/events", body_with_empty_optional_strings, auth_header); assert resp.status_code == 201; assert len(resp.body) == 0; row = db_query("SELECT node_id, session_id, archetype FROM events WHERE id=?", event_id); assert row.node_id == ""; assert row.session_id == ""; assert row.archetype == ""

test_cases.description: Verify that the handler checks that the first non-whitespace byte of payload json.RawMessage is '{', rejecting payload arrays.
preconditions: Service is running with initialised events table.
Valid Bearer token and Content-Type: application/json are used.
events table is empty before the test.
test_cases.input: POST /v1/events with a valid JSON body where payload is an array: {"id":"x","timestamp":"t","run_id":"r","event_type":"e","node_id":"","session_id":"","archetype":"","severity":"info","payload":["not","an","object"]}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 Created with zero-byte body; no row inserted in the events table.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST("/v1/events", body_with_array_payload, auth_header); assert resp.status_code == 201; assert len(resp.body) == 0; count = db_query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events").count; assert count == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that unknown extra top-level fields in the JSON body are silently ignored and the event is still accepted and stored.
preconditions: Service is running with initialised events table.
Valid Bearer token and Content-Type: application/json are used.
events table is empty before the test.
test_cases.input: POST /v1/events with the canonical valid AuditEvent JSON body plus an extra field: {"id":"abc","timestamp":"t","run_id":"r","event_type":"e","node_id":"","session_id":"","archetype":"","severity":"info","payload":{},"extra_field":"ignored"}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 Created; one row stored in events table; no error or rejection due to the extra field.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST("/v1/events", body_with_extra_field, auth_header); assert resp.status_code == 201; assert len(resp.body) == 0; count = db_query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events WHERE id='abc'").count; assert count == 1

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /v1/events returns HTTP 201 with a zero-byte body and no Content-Type header for both valid and invalid (non-empty) event payloads.
preconditions: Service is running with initialised events table.
Valid Bearer token and Content-Type: application/json are used.
test_cases.input: Two requests: (1) canonical valid AuditEvent JSON, (2) invalid JSON body {"bad":"event"} missing all required fields.
test_cases.expected: Both responses return HTTP 201 with zero-byte body and no Content-Type header.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for body in [valid_body, invalid_body]: resp = POST("/v1/events", body, auth_header); assert resp.status_code == 201; assert len(resp.body) == 0; assert resp.headers.get("Content-Type") == None OR ""

test_cases.description: Verify that the events table schema has exactly 10 columns with the correct names, types, and constraints as defined in the DDL.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database is initialised by calling the startup DDL from `internal/db/db.go`.
test_cases.input: Execute `PRAGMA table_info(events)` on the initialised database.
test_cases.expected: Exactly 10 columns returned: id (TEXT, PRIMARY KEY), timestamp (TEXT, NOT NULL), run_id (TEXT, NOT NULL), event_type (TEXT, NOT NULL), node_id (TEXT, NOT NULL), session_id (TEXT, NOT NULL), archetype (TEXT, NOT NULL), severity (TEXT, NOT NULL), payload (TEXT, NOT NULL), received_at (DATETIME, NOT NULL). No ROWID, STRICT, or index options.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: rows = db_exec("PRAGMA table_info(events)"); assert len(rows) == 10; columns = {row.name: row for row in rows}; assert columns["id"].pk == 1; assert columns["timestamp"].notnull == 1; assert columns["run_id"].notnull == 1; assert columns["event_type"].notnull == 1; assert columns["received_at"].type == "DATETIME"; assert columns["received_at"].notnull == 1

test_cases.description: Verify that the event's own id field is used as the primary key in the events table, and no server-side UUID is generated.
preconditions: Service is running with initialised events table.
Valid Bearer token and Content-Type: application/json are used.
events table is empty before the test.
test_cases.input: POST /v1/events with canonical valid AuditEvent JSON with id '7c10ec9b-daaf-4146-8e40-6efc92e5db39'.
test_cases.expected: The events table contains exactly one row where id = '7c10ec9b-daaf-4146-8e40-6efc92e5db39', matching the submitted event's id field.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST("/v1/events", canonical_body, auth_header); assert resp.status_code == 201; row = db_query("SELECT id FROM events WHERE id='7c10ec9b-daaf-4146-8e40-6efc92e5db39'"); assert row != nil; assert row.id == "7c10ec9b-daaf-4146-8e40-6efc92e5db39"

test_cases.description: Verify that the payload column stores re-serialized JSON text produced by json.Marshal on the parsed json.RawMessage, not a verbatim copy of the request bytes.
preconditions: Service is running with initialised events table.
Valid Bearer token and Content-Type: application/json are used.
events table is empty before the test.
test_cases.input: POST /v1/events with canonical valid AuditEvent JSON where payload is {"plan_hash":"abc123"}.
test_cases.expected: The payload column in the events table contains a valid JSON string representing {"plan_hash":"abc123"} (parseable as JSON, semantically equivalent); exact byte order is not asserted.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST("/v1/events", canonical_body, auth_header); row = db_query("SELECT payload FROM events WHERE id=?", event_id); parsed = json_parse(row.payload); assert parsed["plan_hash"] == "abc123"

test_cases.description: Verify that received_at is stored as a server-generated UTC timestamp formatted with time.RFC3339Nano.
preconditions: Service is running with initialised events table.
Valid Bearer token and Content-Type: application/json are used.
events table is empty before the test.
test_cases.input: POST /v1/events with canonical valid AuditEvent JSON.
test_cases.expected: The received_at column contains a non-empty string parseable as RFC3339Nano (e.g. '2026-06-18T11:26:26.123456789Z'); the parsed time is a UTC timestamp close to the time of insertion.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST("/v1/events", canonical_body, auth_header); row = db_query("SELECT received_at FROM events WHERE id=?", event_id); assert row.received_at != ""; parsed_time = time_parse(time.RFC3339Nano, row.received_at); assert parsed_time.error == nil; assert parsed_time.location == UTC

test_cases.description: Verify that on startup the service executes CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events with the new 10-column schema.
preconditions: A fresh (empty) SQLite database file is provided.
`internal/db/db.go` InitDB function (or equivalent startup function) is called.
test_cases.input: Call the DB initialization function from `internal/db/db.go` on an empty SQLite database.
test_cases.expected: The events table is created with 10 columns as per the authoritative DDL; PRAGMA table_info(events) returns 10 rows.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = open_sqlite(empty_db_path); init_db(db); rows = db_exec("PRAGMA table_info(events)"); assert len(rows) == 10

test_cases.description: Verify that the startup DDL does not contain any DROP TABLE statement.
preconditions: Source file `internal/db/db.go` is accessible.
test_cases.input: Read the source of `internal/db/db.go` and search for DROP TABLE.
test_cases.expected: No occurrence of DROP TABLE (case-insensitive) exists anywhere in `internal/db/db.go`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: source = read_file("internal/db/db.go"); assert "DROP TABLE" not in source.to_upper_case()

test_cases.description: Verify that submitting a valid event with a duplicate id is silently ignored: InsertEvent returns nil, and the handler returns 201 Created with a zero-byte body.
preconditions: Service is running with initialised events table.
Valid Bearer token and Content-Type: application/json are used.
The canonical valid AuditEvent has already been successfully inserted once.
test_cases.input: POST /v1/events with the same canonical valid AuditEvent JSON a second time (same id).
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 Created with zero-byte body and no Content-Type header; exactly one row with that id remains in the events table.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: POST("/v1/events", canonical_body, auth_header); resp2 = POST("/v1/events", canonical_body, auth_header); assert resp2.status_code == 201; assert len(resp2.body) == 0; count = db_query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events WHERE id=?", event_id).count; assert count == 1

test_cases.description: Verify that InsertEvent has the correct function signature: func InsertEvent(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB, event AuditEvent) error in the db package.
preconditions: Source file `internal/db/events.go` compiles successfully.
test_cases.input: Inspect the function signature of InsertEvent in `internal/db/events.go`.
test_cases.expected: The function is exported as InsertEvent, accepts (ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB, event AuditEvent), and returns a single error value.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert function_signature(db.InsertEvent) == "func(context.Context, *sql.DB, db.AuditEvent) error"; assert package_of(db.InsertEvent) == "db"

test_cases.description: Verify that InsertEvent executes INSERT OR IGNORE INTO events with all 9 event fields and a server-generated received_at timestamp using a bare db.ExecContext call.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database with the 10-column events table schema is initialised.
A valid AuditEvent struct is constructed in memory.
test_cases.input: Call InsertEvent(ctx, db, validAuditEvent) directly.
test_cases.expected: Returns nil error; the events table contains one row with all 9 event columns matching the input struct and received_at set to a recent UTC RFC3339Nano timestamp.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: event = AuditEvent{ID:"test-id", Timestamp:"t", RunID:"r", EventType:"e", NodeID:"", SessionID:"", Archetype:"", Severity:"info", Payload:json_raw("{}") }; err = db.InsertEvent(ctx, sqldb, event); assert err == nil; row = db_query("SELECT * FROM events WHERE id='test-id'"); assert row.event_type == "e"; assert row.severity == "info"; parsed_time = time_parse(time.RFC3339Nano, row.received_at); assert parsed_time.error == nil

test_cases.description: Verify that the EventsHandler accesses the database via h.DB using the existing struct injection pattern, with no new injection mechanism.
preconditions: Source file `internal/handler/events.go` compiles successfully.
test_cases.input: Inspect the EventsHandler struct definition and handler method for h.DB usage.
test_cases.expected: EventsHandler struct has a DB *sql.DB field; the handler method uses h.DB to call InsertEvent; no new injection mechanism (e.g. context injection or global variable) is introduced.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert field_exists(handler.EventsHandler, "DB", "*sql.DB"); assert handler_method_uses("h.DB"); assert no_new_injection_mechanism_in("internal/handler/events.go")

test_cases.description: Verify that existing behaviours (Bearer auth, Content-Type validation, empty body 400, health checks, graceful shutdown) remain unchanged after the schema enforcement changes.
preconditions: Service is running with the updated code and 10-column events table.
Both valid and invalid auth tokens are available for testing.
test_cases.input: Multiple requests: (1) POST /v1/events with no auth token, (2) POST /v1/events with wrong Content-Type, (3) POST /v1/events with empty body, (4) GET /healthz, (5) GET /readyz.
test_cases.expected: (1) 401 Unauthorized, (2) 415 or equivalent Content-Type error, (3) 400 Bad Request, (4) 200 OK for healthz, (5) 200 OK for readyz.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert POST("/v1/events", body, no_auth).status == 401; assert POST("/v1/events", body, wrong_content_type).status in [400, 415]; assert POST("/v1/events", empty_body, auth).status == 400; assert GET("/healthz").status == 200; assert GET("/readyz").status == 200

test_cases.description: TestTS02_1: POST canonical valid AuditEvent, assert 201 with zero-byte body, query DB and verify all 9 event columns match; assert received_at is parseable as RFC3339Nano.
preconditions: Service is running with initialised 10-column events table (empty before test).
Valid Bearer token and Content-Type: application/json are used.
test_cases.input: POST /v1/events with canonical valid AuditEvent: {"id":"7c10ec9b-daaf-4146-8e40-6efc92e5db39","timestamp":"2026-06-18T11:26:26.527713+00:00","run_id":"20260618_112626_17dd3f","event_type":"run.start","node_id":"","session_id":"","archetype":"","severity":"info","payload":{"plan_hash":"abc123"}}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with zero-byte body and no Content-Type header; DB row has id='7c10ec9b-daaf-4146-8e40-6efc92e5db39', timestamp='2026-06-18T11:26:26.527713+00:00', run_id='20260618_112626_17dd3f', event_type='run.start', node_id='', session_id='', archetype='', severity='info', payload parseable as JSON with plan_hash='abc123', received_at parseable as RFC3339Nano.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST("/v1/events", canonical_body, auth_header); assert resp.status_code == 201; assert len(resp.body) == 0; assert resp.headers.get("Content-Type") == None; row = db_query("SELECT * FROM events WHERE id='7c10ec9b-daaf-4146-8e40-6efc92e5db39'"); assert row.timestamp == "2026-06-18T11:26:26.527713+00:00"; assert row.run_id == "20260618_112626_17dd3f"; assert row.event_type == "run.start"; assert row.node_id == ""; assert row.session_id == ""; assert row.archetype == ""; assert row.severity == "info"; assert json_parse(row.payload)["plan_hash"] == "abc123"; assert time_parse(RFC3339Nano, row.received_at).error == nil

test_cases.description: TestTS02_2: POST three invalid payloads (missing event_type, array payload, empty id); assert 201 for each and verify no rows stored.
preconditions: Service is running with initialised 10-column events table (empty before test).
Valid Bearer token and Content-Type: application/json are used.
test_cases.input: Three separate POST /v1/events requests: (a) missing event_type field, (b) payload is an array ["not","an","object"], (c) id is an empty string "".
test_cases.expected: All three responses return HTTP 201 with zero-byte body; events table remains empty (0 rows) after all three requests.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for body in [body_missing_event_type, body_array_payload, body_empty_id]: resp = POST("/v1/events", body, auth_header); assert resp.status_code == 201; assert len(resp.body) == 0; count = db_query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events").count; assert count == 0

test_cases.description: TestTS02_3: POST canonical valid AuditEvent twice with the same id; assert 201 both times and verify exactly one row in the DB.
preconditions: Service is running with initialised 10-column events table (empty before test).
Valid Bearer token and Content-Type: application/json are used.
test_cases.input: POST /v1/events twice with identical canonical valid AuditEvent JSON (same id '7c10ec9b-daaf-4146-8e40-6efc92e5db39').
test_cases.expected: Both responses return HTTP 201 with zero-byte body; exactly one row with id='7c10ec9b-daaf-4146-8e40-6efc92e5db39' exists in the events table.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp1 = POST("/v1/events", canonical_body, auth_header); assert resp1.status_code == 201; resp2 = POST("/v1/events", canonical_body, auth_header); assert resp2.status_code == 201; count = db_query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events WHERE id='7c10ec9b-daaf-4146-8e40-6efc92e5db39'").count; assert count == 1

test_cases.description: Verify that TestTS01_1, TestTS01_2, and TestTS01_5 in internal/integration/events_test.go are updated to send the canonical valid AuditEvent JSON payload with distinct id values.
preconditions: Source file `internal/integration/events_test.go` is accessible.
Service is running with initialised 10-column events table.
test_cases.input: Inspect and run TestTS01_1, TestTS01_2, TestTS01_5 with the canonical valid AuditEvent payloads.
test_cases.expected: Each test sends a valid AuditEvent JSON body (all 9 required fields, correct types, non-empty required strings, object payload) and receives HTTP 201; distinct id values are used across the three tests.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for test_name in ["TestTS01_1", "TestTS01_2", "TestTS01_5"]: run_test(test_name); verify_payload_is_canonical_audit_event(test_name); ids = [get_id_from_payload(t) for t in tests]; assert len(set(ids)) == len(ids)

property_tests.description: For any non-empty POST /v1/events body (valid, invalid, or arbitrary), the HTTP response is always 201 Created with a zero-byte body and no Content-Type header.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary non-empty byte slices as request bodies, including: valid canonical AuditEvent JSON, JSON missing required fields, JSON with wrong types, JSON arrays, JSON scalars, malformed JSON, and random byte sequences.
property_tests.invariant_check: FOR ALL non_empty_body: resp = POST("/v1/events", non_empty_body, valid_auth_header, content_type_json); ASSERT resp.status_code == 201 AND len(resp.body) == 0 AND resp.headers.get("Content-Type") == None

property_tests.description: For any event that fails validation (bad JSON, missing required field, empty required string, wrong type, non-object payload), no row is inserted into the events table.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate invalid AuditEvent bodies by: omitting each of the 9 required fields one at a time, setting each non-empty required string field to empty, setting payload to array/null/string/number, sending malformed JSON, sending JSON arrays/scalars at top level.
property_tests.invariant_check: FOR ALL invalid_body: count_before = db_query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events").count; POST("/v1/events", invalid_body, auth); count_after = db_query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events").count; ASSERT count_after == count_before

property_tests.description: For any valid AuditEvent submitted N times with the same id, exactly one row with that id exists in the events table after all N submissions.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate valid AuditEvent structs with a fixed id; submit them N times (N in range 1..10) via POST /v1/events.
property_tests.invariant_check: FOR ALL valid_event, N in 1..10: FOR i in 1..N: POST("/v1/events", valid_event_body, auth); count = db_query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events WHERE id=?", valid_event.id).count; ASSERT count == 1

property_tests.description: For any valid AuditEvent successfully stored, all 10 columns are NOT NULL and match the submitted event values, with received_at parseable as RFC3339Nano.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate valid AuditEvent structs with varied field values (non-empty id, timestamp, run_id, event_type, severity; any string for node_id/session_id/archetype; any valid JSON object for payload).
property_tests.invariant_check: FOR ALL valid_event: POST("/v1/events", valid_event_body, auth); row = db_query("SELECT * FROM events WHERE id=?", valid_event.id); ASSERT row.id == valid_event.id AND row.timestamp == valid_event.timestamp AND row.run_id == valid_event.run_id AND row.event_type == valid_event.event_type AND row.node_id == valid_event.node_id AND row.session_id == valid_event.session_id AND row.archetype == valid_event.archetype AND row.severity == valid_event.severity AND json_parse(row.payload) semantically_equals json_parse(valid_event.payload) AND time_parse(RFC3339Nano, row.received_at).error == nil AND row.received_at != NULL

property_tests.description: For any valid AuditEvent JSON body containing additional unknown top-level fields beyond the 9 defined, the event is accepted and stored; unknown fields do not cause rejection.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate valid canonical AuditEvent JSON bodies augmented with 1-5 additional top-level fields of arbitrary names and values (strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, objects).
property_tests.invariant_check: FOR ALL valid_event_with_extra_fields: count_before = db_query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events").count; resp = POST("/v1/events", body, auth); ASSERT resp.status_code == 201; count_after = db_query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events").count; ASSERT count_after == count_before + 1

property_tests.description: For any startup attempt where the CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS DDL fails, the service process exits with a non-zero code and never begins accepting requests.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Simulate DDL failures by providing database handles configured to return errors on exec (read-only DB, corrupted DB, disk-full simulation).
property_tests.invariant_check: FOR ALL failing_db_handle: assert process_exits_nonzero( func() { init_db(failing_db_handle) } ) AND assert no_http_server_started()

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that extra top-level fields beyond the 9 defined in AuditEvent are silently ignored and the event is accepted and stored.
preconditions: Service is running with initialised 10-column events table (empty before test).
Valid Bearer token and Content-Type: application/json are used.
edge_case_tests.input: POST /v1/events with canonical valid AuditEvent JSON plus additional fields: {"id":"extra-field-id","timestamp":"t","run_id":"r","event_type":"e","node_id":"","session_id":"","archetype":"","severity":"info","payload":{},"future_field":"value","another_extra":42}
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 201 Created; one row stored in events with id='extra-field-id'; no rejection due to extra fields.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST("/v1/events", body_with_extra_fields, auth_header); assert resp.status_code == 201; assert len(resp.body) == 0; count = db_query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events WHERE id='extra-field-id'").count; assert count == 1

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a non-empty body with malformed JSON is silently rejected with HTTP 201 and no row is stored.
preconditions: Service is running with initialised 10-column events table (empty before test).
Valid Bearer token and Content-Type: application/json are used.
edge_case_tests.input: POST /v1/events with body: 'this is not json'
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 201 Created with zero-byte body and no Content-Type header; no row inserted in events table.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST("/v1/events", "this is not json", auth_header); assert resp.status_code == 201; assert len(resp.body) == 0; assert resp.headers.get("Content-Type") == None; count = db_query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events").count; assert count == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a non-empty body that is a JSON array (not an object) is silently rejected with HTTP 201 and no row is stored.
preconditions: Service is running with initialised 10-column events table (empty before test).
Valid Bearer token and Content-Type: application/json are used.
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 201 Created with zero-byte body and no Content-Type header; no row inserted in events table.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST("/v1/events", "[\"not\", \"an\", \"object\"]", auth_header); assert resp.status_code == 201; assert len(resp.body) == 0; count = db_query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events").count; assert count == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a non-empty body that is a JSON scalar (null) is silently rejected with HTTP 201 and no row is stored.
preconditions: Service is running with initialised 10-column events table (empty before test).
Valid Bearer token and Content-Type: application/json are used.
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 201 Created with zero-byte body and no Content-Type header; no row inserted in events table.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST("/v1/events", "null", auth_header); assert resp.status_code == 201; assert len(resp.body) == 0; count = db_query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events").count; assert count == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that an event with an empty string id is silently rejected with HTTP 201 and no row is stored.
preconditions: Service is running with initialised 10-column events table (empty before test).
Valid Bearer token and Content-Type: application/json are used.
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 201 Created with zero-byte body; no row inserted in events table.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST("/v1/events", body_empty_id, auth_header); assert resp.status_code == 201; assert len(resp.body) == 0; count = db_query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events").count; assert count == 0

edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 201 Created with zero-byte body; no row inserted in events table.

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that an event whose payload first non-whitespace byte is '[' (array) is silently rejected with HTTP 201 and no row is stored.

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that an empty request body returns HTTP 400 Bad Request, preserving existing middleware behavior.
preconditions: Service is running with initialised events table.
Valid Bearer token and Content-Type: application/json are used.
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400 Bad Request; no change to events table.

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that if CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS DDL fails on startup, the service logs a fatal error and exits with a non-zero exit code.
preconditions: A database handle is configured such that DDL execution will fail (e.g., a read-only database file or an in-memory DB that returns an error on exec).
edge_case_tests.input: Call the DB initialization function from `internal/db/db.go` with a database handle that returns an error on DDL execution.
edge_case_tests.expected: The function logs a fatal error and calls os.Exit (or log.Fatal) with a non-zero exit code; it does not return normally.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mock_db = db_that_fails_on_exec(); assert panics_or_exits_nonzero( func() { init_db(mock_db) } )

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that if the events table already exists (even with the old 3-column schema), CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS is a no-op and the service starts normally.
preconditions: A SQLite database file exists with the old 3-column events table schema (id, payload, received_at).
edge_case_tests.input: Call the DB initialization function from `internal/db/db.go` on the database with the old schema.
edge_case_tests.expected: The function returns without error; no fatal exit occurs; the service continues startup normally.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: db = open_sqlite(old_schema_db_path); err = init_db(db); assert err == nil OR no_exit_called()

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the happy path: a valid audit event is authenticated, validated, stored in the events table, and a 201 response is returned.
smoke_tests.trigger: POST /v1/events with canonical valid AuditEvent JSON, valid Bearer token, and Content-Type: application/json.
expected_effects: HTTP 201 Created with zero-byte body and no Content-Type header
One row inserted into the events table with all 9 event fields and a valid received_at timestamp

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the silent rejection path: an invalid event is received, silently rejected without storage, and a 201 response is returned.
smoke_tests.trigger: POST /v1/events with an invalid JSON body (e.g. missing event_type field), valid Bearer token, and Content-Type: application/json.
expected_effects: HTTP 201 Created with zero-byte body and no Content-Type header
No row inserted into the events table
No error logged for the rejected event

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the duplicate event path: the same valid event submitted twice results in only one stored row and 201 for both requests.
smoke_tests.trigger: POST /v1/events twice with identical canonical valid AuditEvent JSON (same id), valid Bearer token, and Content-Type: application/json.
real_components: InsertEvent function with INSERT OR IGNORE (internal/db/events.go)
expected_effects: HTTP 201 Created with zero-byte body for both requests
Exactly one row exists in the events table with the submitted id

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for successful service startup: the service executes CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS DDL and begins serving requests.
smoke_tests.trigger: Start the service binary with a fresh (empty) SQLite database file.
expected_effects: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events DDL executes successfully
events table exists with 10 columns per PRAGMA table_info(events)
Service begins accepting HTTP requests (GET /healthz returns 200)

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for startup failure: if the CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS DDL fails, the service logs a fatal error and exits with a non-zero exit code.
smoke_tests.trigger: Start the service with a database configuration that causes the DDL statement to fail (e.g. read-only database file).
mockable: SQLite database handle (configured to fail on DDL exec)
expected_effects: Service logs a fatal error message referencing the DDL failure
Process exits with a non-zero exit code
No HTTP server is started; no requests are served
